NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO COMPEL COMPLIANCE WITH DEPOSITION SUBPOENA OF LIAM DAY AND PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS (ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR TENTATIVE RULING ENTRY PURPOSES ONLY)
Off Calendar Feb-15-2023 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Compliance With Deposition Subpoena Of Liam Day And Production Of Documents
Off Calendar Feb-15-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):

Atty. Jamie Couche, Esq. of ANDERSON POOLE& COUCHE, P.C. (Tele: 415-956-6413) for plaintiff;
Atty. Ronald Houseman, Esq. of Law Offices of Vincent Martin Spohn (Tele: 707-255-2885) for Defendant

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF ALAN BUCKLEY, DANIELLE JACKSON Motion To Compel Compliance With Deposition Subpoena Of Liam Day And Production Of Documents

The parties to the motion stipulate to the matter being decided by the pro tem. The pro tem judge and counsel for the parties to the motion authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

No Contest filed. The Court adopts its tentative ruling:

Motion to compel GRANTED. By failing to timely file objections to the deposition subpoena, submit a privilege log, or assert objections during the deposition, both Day and the other Defendants waived any potential privilege claims. Regarding the question about the number of emails between Day and Whiteside, the joint defense privilege was asserted, but a question about the number of emails is permissible in a way that questions about the contents of those emails might not be. Plaintiffs have the better argument regarding the litigation privilege: it provides a defense to liability not a privilege against production. The deposition testimony regarding how Day searched demonstrates that it was not an adequate search across all requested communication tools. Complete production of all responsive materials (text messages, emails, etc.) due no later than March 1, 2023, unless otherwise agreed by the parties. Day must sit for deposition within 10 days of production unless otherwise agreed by the parties. Sanctions GRANTED in the amount of $5,625. The requested hourly rate is consistent with practice in this court and the requested hours are reasonable. Enforcement of sanctions is STAYED. If Day completes production and sits for deposition as set out above, sanctions will be waived.

Order signed after hearing.

Judge Pro Tem: Scott Borrowman; Clerk: R Sahatjian; Not Reported =(302/JPT)
